LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PERMA-PIPE, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. JUDGE KAHN MADE A PERSONAL DISCLOSURE. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE NEGLIGENCE CLAIM IS ADEQUATELY PLED. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO ALLEGE, IF IT CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, THAT THE ALLEGED EXPRESS WARRANTY/STATEMENT WAS PART OF AN AGREEMENT BETWEEN IT AND PERMA-PIPE AND THAT IT WAS EITHER IN PRIVITY OF CONTRACT OR HAD DIRECT DEALINGS WITH PERMA-PIPE. THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS DENIED BECAUSE THE DOCUMENT SOUGHT TO BE JUDICIALLY NOTICED IS NOT ONE THAT MAY BE JUDICIALLY NOTICED. ALSO THE REGENTS ARE GRANTED LEAVE TO ALLEGE ADDITIONAL CAUSE OF ACTION FOR STRICT LIABILITY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-30-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PERMA-PIPE, INC.'s, Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint is granted. However, on the court's own motion all of Perma-Pipe's cross-complaint is severed from the complaint filed by Travelers against Perma-Pipe and all litigation activity on the cross-complaint, including service of the cross-complaint on the cross-defendants, is stayed until the resolution of the complaint filed by Travelers and Perma-Pipe in this court. If and when the stay is lifted and the cross-complaint is served, cross-defendants will be permitted to raise any substantive arguments that were raised in opposition to this motion by way of demurrer. The policy of liberal allowance of amendments to pleadings and the carve-out in the settlement agreement for causes of action and claims arising out of the Travelers claim favor the granting of this motion. The insufficiently explained delay by Perma-Pipe in filing this motion, the fact that the trial date is less than four months away, the reasonable likelihood that cross-defendants' motion practice and discovery could not be completed before the trial date, and judicial economy all favor the severance and stay of the cross-complaint. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

DEFENDANT-ININTERVENTION PERMA-PIPE, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION as to all six claims alleged in Travelers' complaint in intervention is OVERRULED as to all six claims. Travelers has adequately alleged each of its six claims. The additional insured endorsement in Bass' policy does not preclude Perma-Pipe from having a contractual duty to defend and indemnify Ranger (the first four claims) or Travelers being equitably or contractually subrogated to Ranger's rights vis-a-vis Perma-Pipe. Perma-Pipe's request for judicial notice as to Travelers' complaint against Ranger and Perma-Pipe's customer order acknowledgment is DENIED. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

At the conclusion of the hearing counsel make a joint oral request for trial continuance. The Court directs counsel to confer and come up with a schedule that works for both sides and to submit a stipulated [proposed] order to continue trial directly to this court with a reminder cover letter.

Off Calendar Aug-24-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Peter W. Dye of Koeller Nebeker Carlson Haluck LLP (916-724-5700) appears as counsel for cross-defendant/moving party, Bay Area Systems & Solutions Inc.

Attorney, Brent F. Basilico Sellar Hazard & Lucia (925-938-1430) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party, Ranger Pipelines

Attorney, Mark W. Prager of Lee Hernandez Landrum Garofalo (925-939-2000) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party, Ranger Pipelines

Attorney, Erin E. Krejci of Laurie & Brennan LLP (312-445-8785) appears telephonically as counsel for defendant/cross-defendant, Perna-Pipe Inc. et al

Attorney, Kimberly R. Arnal of Aguilera Law Group (714-384-6600), telephonically for Plaintiff In Intervention, Travelers Property Casualty Company

Attorney, Victoria Prange of Collinsworth Specht Calkins & Giampaoli (925-400-9575) appears telephonically as counsel for defendant/opposing party, Ranger Pipelines

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. Both sides' requests for ruling on evidentiary objections granted. Mr. Basilico's evidentiary objections ruled upon in open court; Mr. Dye's evidentiary objections may be submitted via email.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

CROSS-DEFENDANT BAY AREA SYSTEMS AND SOLUTIONS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED in its entirety. While the cross-complaint and amended cross-complaint are similar, the motion challenges claims in the cross-complaint, which has been superseded by the amended cross-complaint. However, even surpassing that procedural defect, the motion must be denied because there is a triable issue whether the purchase order ("PO") and purchase order agreement ("POA") govern the parties' relationship. Viewing the evidence in a light most favorable to cross-complainant Ranger Pipelines, Inc., the time and materials document ("T&M") was a rates and fees proposal and Mr. Grover merely said "OK." The unintegrated T&M does not show that the parties intended the T&M to be their entire agreement and Mr. Grover's declaration at paragraph 8 states otherwise. In addition, the PO, which was sent to Bass Electric after Mr. Grover approved of the T&M, provides: "This Purchase Order supersedes all past quotations in reference to the purchases contained herein within" and "By providing services or shipment of goods you are bound by the conditions and terms contained herein." Paragraphs 9-11 of Mr. Grover's declaration create a triable issue whether Bass Electric ever objected to this PO and manifested its assent in accordance with the PO by performance. C9 Ventures v. SVC-West, L.P., (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 1483 is distinguishable because the invoice in that case did not contain language similar to the PO. C9 Ventures at 1500 recognizes that under the common law, assent can be manifested in ways other than a signature. Mr. Grover communicated to Mr. Alvarez that the PO was required and Mr. Alvarez never voiced an objection. (Grover Dec., par. 13.) The PO required that Mr. Grover must authorize adjustments and the parties acted in accordance with that provision. (Grover Dec., par. 12.) (So. Cal. Edison Co. v. Sup. Crt. (1995) 37 Cal.App.4th 839, 851 ("The rule is well-settled that in construing the terms of a contract the construction given it by the acts and conduct of the parties with knowledge of its terms, and before any controversy has arisen as to its meaning, is admissible on the issue of the parties' intent.")) The Court shall issue its own written order.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, bus. tel: (530) 339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-22-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Roland Nikles, Esq., from Rogers Joseph O'Donnell, 415-956-2828, for defendant and moving party Perma-Pipe, Inc.
Matthew A. Richards, Esq., from Nixon Peabody LLP, 415-984-5093, for plaintiff and opposing party
Roger F. Liu, Esq., from Leonidou & Rosin, 650-691-2888, for defendant Ranger Pipeline, appearing by telephone

Judge Kahn made a disclosure about being a good fried with the General Counsel for the Regents, but he believes that it does not have any effect on his ability to be fair and impartial. No objections were made.
The matter was argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PERMA-PIPE, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.
Overruled as to the first cause of action and sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the third and fourth causes of action. The negligence claim is adequately pled. Plaintiff is granted leave to allege, if it can do so in good faith, that the alleged express warranty/statement was part of an agreement between it and Perma-Pipe and that it was either in privity of contract or had direct dealings with Perma-Pipe. The request for judicial notice is denied because the document sought to be judicially noticed is not one that may be judicially noticed. Also The Regents are granted leave to allege additional cause of action for strict liability. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077
